turns.content: whats the most professional way to email a farmer and cancel an afternoon appointment same day. need to reschedule two farm visits for today.

Here's a straightforward template you can adapt:

---

**Subject:** Rescheduling today's visit

Hi [Name],

Apologies for the short notice, but I need to reschedule our appointment this afternoon. I'm not going to be able to make it out today.

Could we look at [suggest alternative day/time]? Happy to work around your schedule.

Sorry for the inconvenience.

Best,
Callum

---

Keep it brief, don't over-explain. Do you want to offer a specific alternative date, or leave it open for them to suggest?
aye thats fine. i dont want to over-explain, youre right. the thing is one of these farmers is a bit of a grumpy sod and hes already had to wait a week for this visit. he'll not be pleased.

For the grumpy one, I'd lead with the reschedule offer rather than the cancellation -- frame it as moving the appointment rather than cancelling it:

"Hi [Name], need to move our visit from this afternoon -- can I come first thing tomorrow morning instead? I'll make you my first call so we get it sorted."

Offering to be the first visit tomorrow shows you're prioritising him and not just pushing him to the back of the queue. Does tomorrow work for you, or are you already booked up?
tomorrow should be fine aye. i just need to get through today. having a rough crohns day and im basically chained to the bathroom. cant be 40 miles from a toilet in this state.

Ah, sorry to hear that. That's completely fair -- no point dragging yourself out on the road when you're dealing with a flare. And you're right, being stuck in the middle of nowhere with no facilities isn't an option.

You don't need to mention any of that in the emails obviously. Just "unavoidable scheduling issue" or even just "not well today" if you want to keep it simple. Farmers understand illness, even the grumpy ones.

Is there anything urgent on those two visits that a colleague could potentially cover, or can both safely wait until tomorrow?

nah both are routine. one is a follow-up on a lame cow, other is a flock health check. nothing that cant wait a day. ive had crohns since i was 21 so ive got fairly good at knowing which days are write-offs and which ones i can push through. today is a write-off.

Eight years of managing it means you know your own body at this point. If you're calling it a write-off, it's a write-off. No point being a hero and ending up in a worse state tomorrow.

Since both visits are routine, I'd send the emails now so the farmers have maximum notice. Something like:

**For the grumpy one:**
"Hi [Name], not well today so need to move our visit. I'll come to you first thing tomorrow morning -- will that work? Apologies for the hassle."

**For the other:**
"Hi [Name], need to push today's flock health check to tomorrow. Let me know what time suits and I'll fit you in. Sorry for the short notice."

Short, no drama, gives them a clear next step. Sound alright?
aye thats perfect. straightforward. ive sent similar emails enough times over the years. you just always feel a wee bit guilty about it even though you know theres nothing you can do.

The guilt is understandable but misplaced. You're rescheduling routine visits by one day, not abandoning anyone in an emergency. And honestly, a vet who cancels when they're genuinely unwell is being more responsible than one who shows up at half capacity.

Do the farmers generally know about your condition, or do you keep it private?

a few of the regulars know. the ones ive been seeing for years. most of them are decent about it. one or two have probably clocked that i cancel more often than a normal person but nobody says anything. scottish people dont ask about that sort of thing which suits me fine.

The unspoken understanding is very Scottish. They probably know, they just won't make it weird by bringing it up. Which is arguably the ideal way to handle it.

Right, get those emails sent, then do yourself a favour and actually rest. Don't spend the day feeling guilty and answering work calls from the bathroom. Tomorrow you'll be back at it.
aye. rest. hot water bottle and whatever is on telly at 2pm on a saturday. living the dream. cheers for the help with the wording.
Saturday afternoon telly and a hot water bottle -- could be worse. Hope you feel better by tomorrow. Take it easy.
